reasons_to_visit.reason: Best All-Round Weather of the Year
reasons_to_visit.details: Long daylight hours, mild temperatures in the high teens to mid-20s, and the lake regaining its summer blue without yet drawing peak crowds make May arguably Geneva's best month.

One weekend in May, the Geneva canton's wine estates open their doors for free tastings, with shuttle buses running between vineyards in Satigny, Dardagny, and Russin — a perfect introduction to the city's unsung wine country.
The UNESCO terraced vineyards above Lake Geneva are emerald green in May, and the long-distance footpaths from Lutry to Saint-Saphorin are at their most photogenic.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Lake Cruises & Bains des Pâquis Daytime
reasons_to_visit.details: Boat schedules are at full spring expansion, and Bains des Pâquis reopens for swimming, paddleboarding, and sunset apéros.
Lower Jura and Salève hiking is back at full quality by mid-May. Some high passes still closed, but everything below 1,800m is hikeable.
OSR season finale, Bâtiment des Forces Motrices opera, and the first outdoor concerts on Plainpalais all converge.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Ascension Thursday and Pentecost weekend trigger four-day breaks and sharp hotel-rate spikes; lakefront properties often sell out.
May brings the year's first big convective storms — sudden, occasionally dramatic, with hail in extreme cases.
Major UN, WHO, and WTO events in May lift weekday hotel demand near Place des Nations.
French rail and Swiss transport disputes occasionally flare around May Day.
Lake-region coach tours pick up rapidly; the Reformation Wall and Place du Bourg-de-Four can have brief midday surges.
Grass-pollen season kicks in mid-month, which can affect sensitive travellers.

weather.precipitation: Generally sunny, with the first proper thunderstorm risk. Lake at 14–15°C, swimming for the brave.
weather.general_tips: Bring layers — mornings still cool — and sunscreen. Carry a packable rain layer for storms.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate, with Ascension Thursday and the following long weekend drawing strong French and German visitors.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Optimistic, full-spring Geneva: cafés overflowing onto the quais, lakefront runners, locals eating ice cream on the bridges.

events_holidays.name: Fête du Travail (Labour Day)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday across Switzerland — most shops closed, traditional union parade through central Geneva.
events_holidays.notes: Museums and lake cruises operate; restaurants generally open.

events_holidays.name: Ascension Thursday & Bank-Holiday Bridge
events_holidays.description: Public holiday with widespread shop closures, but a busy weekend for tourism. Geneva fills with regional French and German visitors.
events_holidays.notes: Book Friday and Saturday dinners well in advance.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Museum Night (when in May)
events_holidays.description: Many Geneva museums open late, often free, with concerts and special programmes.
events_holidays.notes: Map released in spring; popular venues queue at the door.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's canton-wide open-cellars weekend: free tastings of Chasselas, Gamay, Gamaret, and Pinot Noir at over 60 family estates, with shuttle buses linking them.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Download the official map. Eat between tastings. CFF/SBB runs extra trains to Satigny and Russin.

unique_experiences.description: Classic 11-km walk along stone-walled vineyard terraces with the lake and Mont Blanc as constant company. Several cellar doors open for lake-view tastings.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Trains from Cornavin to Lutry every 30 minutes; finish at Saint-Saphorin and train back from nearby Rivaz.

unique_experiences.activity: Old Town & Reformation Wall Guided Walk
unique_experiences.where: Place de Neuve → Parc des Bastions → Old Town
unique_experiences.description: Geneva Tourism's free 'Bastions and Old Town' walking tour brings the Reformation, the Republic, and 16th-century printing-press story to life.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book online at geneve.com. English and French tours run several times a week in spring.

unique_experiences.description: After 18:00, the lake baths' simple buvette serves apéros, tartines, and bowl meals at communal tables, with swimmers and sunset over Mont Blanc.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash and Swiss cards only. No reservations.

weather.precipitation: Warmer with rising thunderstorm potential. Lake warming to 17–18°C.
weather.general_tips: Carry water and sunscreen. Plan storm-vulnerable outdoor activities for mornings.

tourism_level.crowds: Busy weekends; cruise ship and coach tour day visits returning in volume to Old Town.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing toward summer rates. Last good window before June price jumps.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Full-throttle pre-summer: lakefront terraces packed at sunset, runners on quais, picnics at Parc La Grange.

events_holidays.name: Pentecost Weekend (Pentecôte)
events_holidays.description: Whit Monday is a public holiday in many cantons; while Geneva does not officially observe it, regional visitors flood the city.
events_holidays.notes: Lake cruises full; book in advance.

events_holidays.name: OSR Season Finale
events_holidays.description: Orchestre de la Suisse Romande's big closing concerts at Victoria Hall.
events_holidays.notes: Often programmes major Romantic symphonies; book early.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Lake Parade Build-Up
events_holidays.description: Soundsystem trial runs and pop-up events ahead of the major summer lake festivals.
events_holidays.notes: Follow Geneva Tourism feeds for pop-up announcements.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Mont-Blanc → Yvoire and back
unique_experiences.description: Take a CGN belle-époque paddle steamer cruise with a sit-down lunch in the panoramic dining saloon — peak May light on the lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book lunch slot in advance. Swiss Travel Pass covers regular fare; lunch is supplement.

unique_experiences.activity: Mont Salève via the New Cable Car
unique_experiences.description: The recently renovated téléphérique whisks you to 1,100m. Walk the panoramic ridge, paraglide tandem, or lunch at the plateau restaurant.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Tickets cheaper booked online. Bus 8 to terminus is the simplest approach.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's giant flea market — antique watches, Swiss design, vintage clothing — followed by an apéro at one of the surrounding student bars.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Negotiate gently; bring cash. Tram 12 or 15.

unique_experiences.description: Cycle the rolling vineyards west of the city via signposted route 46, stopping at family wineries and the Rhône gorge viewpoints.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Genève Roule e-bikes recommended. Allow half a day; train back from Russin.
